regime_classification.2026-04-08:growth: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","dimension":"growth","metric_summary":"GDP 7.8%; PMI 57","signal_text":"GDP at 7.8% with PMI Composite at 57 signals strong expansion. Manufacturing and services both in growth territory.","badge_label":"Expansion Mode","badge_type":"b-exp"}
regime_classification.2026-04-08:inflation: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","dimension":"inflation","metric_summary":"CPI 3.21%; Fuel -3.78%","signal_text":"CPI at 3.21% with fuel at -3.78%. Inflation within RBI's 2-6% target band — no policy urgency.","badge_label":"Within Band","badge_type":"b-neu"}
regime_classification.2026-04-08:credit: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","dimension":"credit","metric_summary":"Credit 14.3%; CD 83.04%","signal_text":"CD ratio at 83.04% with credit growth at 14.3% — deposits not keeping pace with lending. Funding stress risk for NBFCs and smaller banks unless alternative sources emerge.","badge_label":"Deposit Gap Stress","badge_type":"b-risk"}
regime_classification.2026-04-08:policy: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","dimension":"policy","metric_summary":"Repo 5.25%","signal_text":"Repo rate at 5.25% with RBI in easing cycle. Rate cuts signal accommodative stance — supportive for duration and equities.","badge_label":"Easing Cycle","badge_type":"b-exp"}
regime_classification.2026-04-08:capex: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","dimension":"capex","metric_summary":"IIP CapG 12.5%%; CU 74.80%%","signal_text":"IIP capital goods at 12.5%% with capacity utilisation at 74.80%%. Investment holding steady with no acceleration or deceleration.","badge_label":"Steady Investment","badge_type":"b-neu"}
regime_classification.2026-04-08:consumption: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","dimension":"consumption","metric_summary":"GST 200064; PV 10.5%","signal_text":"GST collections at 200064 YoY with PV sales at 10.5% signals demand surge. Consumer spending broad-based across durables and non-durables.","badge_label":"Demand Surge","badge_type":"b-exp"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:growth: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"growth","metric_summary":"GDP 7.8%; PMI 56.5","signal_text":"GDP at 7.8% and PMI composite at 56.5 signal expansion — until you decompose it: core sector output collapsed to 2.3% YoY from 4.7%, a 240bps miss that covers cement, steel, refinery, and electricity — the actual sinew of the economy. PMI manufacturing fell to 53.9 from 56.9, with new export orders declining for three consecutive months; the composite headline is being held up by services at 57.5, which is real but narrower than it looks. Invert the bull case: if GVA growth is running 150-200bps below GDP due to the statistical discrepancy and deflator assumptions, actual output expansion may be closer to 6.0-6.3% — respectable, but not the 7.8% that justifies current equity multiples.","badge_label":"Expansion Mode","badge_type":"b-exp"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:inflation: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"inflation","metric_summary":"CPI 3.21%; Fuel -3.78%%","signal_text":"CPI at 3.21% sits comfortably within the 2-6% band, but the composition is deteriorating: food inflation jumped to 3.47% from 2.13% — a 134bps sequential surge — while core CPI holds at 3.4%, meaning the disinflationary impulse from goods is exhausted. Fuel at -3.78% is a statistical artifact of administered pricing; with Brent at $98.26/bbl, fuel inflation turns positive by Q1 FY27 and adds 40-60bps to headline CPI with a two-quarter lag. Munger's inversion: RBI's 4.6% inflation forecast (revised up from 4.2%) implies they already see this — a repo rate at 5.25% with real rates at 205bps positive is not accommodative, but it is not tight enough to absorb a commodity shock.","badge_label":"Within Band","badge_type":"b-neu"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:credit: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"credit","metric_summary":"Credit 13.8%; CD 83.04%","signal_text":"Credit-deposit divergence is the single most important structural stress in India's financial system today: bank credit growing at 13.8% YoY against deposit growth of 10.8% — a 300bps gap — has pushed the CD ratio to 83.04%, the 81st percentile of its 10-year range. NBFC credit growth decelerated sharply to 13% from 17% YoY, confirming wholesale funding strain; corporate bond issuance at ₹1,12,000 cr (up from ₹1,06,000 cr) is the pressure valve — banks rationing credit, corporates going direct to market. Historical precedent: the last time CD ratio sustained above 80% during a rate-cut cycle was Q2 FY14; within 12 months, the taper tantrum forced RBI into 75bps of emergency hikes — today's FX reserve buffer is $100bn larger, but the structural fragility rhymes.","badge_label":"Deposit Gap Stress","badge_type":"b-risk"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:policy: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"policy","metric_summary":"Repo 5.25%%","signal_text":"Repo at 5.25% with CPI at 3.21% implies a real policy rate of +204bps — among the tightest in India's post-COVID history and materially above the RBI's own neutral rate estimate of ~150bps real. For housing, the average home loan rate falling to 8.0% from 9.1% is already transmitting — affordability index improved to 0.61 from 0.46 — but corporate capex IRRs remain compressed when 10-year G-Sec yields price in no near-term cuts. Mishra's lens: RBI's GDP forecast at 6.9% vs market consensus at 7.6% is a 70bps gap that signals the central bank is more cautious than the equity market — watch whether this divergence closes via a growth downgrade or a policy pivot.","badge_label":"Rate Pause","badge_type":"b-neu"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:capex: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"capex","metric_summary":"IIP CapG 12.5%%; CU Not available in search results%","signal_text":"IIP capital goods surging to 12.5% YoY from 4.3% is the most misleading number in today's dataset: core sector — which captures actual physical output of coal, steel, cement, electricity, and refinery products — collapsed to 2.3% from 4.7%, a 240bps deceleration that cannot be reconciled with genuine capex acceleration. GST collections on capital goods embedded in the ₹2,00,640 cr monthly total show lumpy, project-driven demand — one large infrastructure disbursement can swing IIP capital goods by 800-1000bps in a single month. Munger's inversion: without capacity utilisation data, we cannot confirm whether private capex is responding to demand signals or merely replacing depreciated assets — the absence of this number is itself a signal that the bull case on investment is unverifiable.","badge_label":"Steady Investment","badge_type":"b-neu"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:consumption: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"consumption","metric_summary":"GST 200640; PV 26.1%","signal_text":"GST at ₹2,00,640 cr crossing the ₹2 lakh cr threshold for the first time is structurally significant — but Mishra's question applies: is this genuine demand expansion or formalization reclassifying existing informal spend? Vehicle sales provide the triangulation: PV at +26.1% YoY, two-wheelers at +28.68%, and commercial vehicles at +28.89% — all three segments accelerating simultaneously, which historically signals broad-based demand, not just urban discretionary. Invert: airline passenger traffic fell to 35.08 mn from 38.59 mn and e-commerce GMV growth decelerated to 12.4% from 25% — the urban, digital, high-frequency consumer is slowing while the physical, semi-urban consumer is accelerating, a divergence that predicts rural income recovery but also suggests the premium consumption story is plateauing.","badge_label":"Demand Surge","badge_type":"b-exp"}

signal_cards.2026-04-08:1: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":1,"signal_theme":"CREDIT CYCLE","status":"risk","title":"Deposit Gap Widens—Funding Stress Ahead","data_text":"CD ratio at 83.04% (prev: 82.38%), bank credit growth at 14.3% vs. deposit growth at 10.9%. Credit-to-deposit gap widening materially. NBFC credit growth holding at 15-17% despite deposit constraints.","implication":"Banks and NBFCs face structural funding pressure. Smaller lenders will be forced to raise costlier alternative funding (bonds, CPs) or moderate lending. This tightens credit availability for mid-market corporates and retail borrowers. Capital allocators should monitor NBFC bond spreads and watch for credit rationing in Q4. Duration plays face headwind if funding costs rise.","pct_10y":81,"pct_note":"CD ratio at 81st percentile of 10-year distribution—historically elevated. Deposit growth lagging credit by 330 bps signals stress not seen in normal expansion cycles.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:2: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":2,"signal_theme":"CAPEX TRIGGER","status":"watch","title":"Capex Steady but No Acceleration Signal","data_text":"IIP capital goods at 12.5% YoY (prev: 4.3%), capacity utilisation at 74.80% (prev: 75.80%). Core sector growth slowed to 2.3% YoY (prev: 4.7%). RE launches at 126.265 units (prev: 123.835), office absorption strong at 83.3 (prev: 73).","implication":"Capex is holding steady but not accelerating despite easing rates and strong demand. Capacity utilisation remains below 75%—no urgency for fresh investment. Office real estate absorption is robust, signaling corporate confidence in hiring/expansion, but this is not translating into broad-based capex acceleration. Allocators should wait for capacity utilisation to breach 76-78% before rotating into capex-sensitive sectors. Current momentum is demand-driven, not investment-driven.","pct_10y":50,"pct_note":"IIP capgoods volatility is high; 12.5% is mid-cycle. Core sector deceleration to 2.3% suggests capex impulse is fading despite strong PMI.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:3: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":3,"signal_theme":"SIP / RETAIL FLOWS","status":"positive","title":"Retail Equity Flows Resilient Despite Volatility","data_text":"SIP inflows at ₹29,845 Cr (prev: ₹31,002 Cr, -3.7% MoM), SIP YoY growth at 15%, SIP accounts at 9.92 Cr (prev: 9.79 Cr), SIP AUM at ₹16.64 Tr (prev: ₹16.36 Tr). Equity MF net inflows at ₹25,978 Cr (prev: ₹24,029 Cr). MF AUM at ₹82.03 Tr (prev: ₹80.24 Tr). NFO collections surged to ₹3,955 Cr (prev: ₹806 Cr).","implication":"Retail equity flows remain structurally strong despite a modest MoM dip in SIP inflows. Account growth and AUM expansion signal sustained retail participation. NFO collections 4.9x higher MoM indicate fresh retail capital entering markets. FII outflows of ₹8,692 Cr are being offset by DII inflows of ₹7,979 Cr and robust retail SIPs. This is a structural floor under equity markets. Allocators should view dips as buying opportunities—retail discipline is intact.","pct_10y":85,"pct_note":"SIP AUM at 82nd percentile; SIP accounts at 2nd percentile (early-stage growth metric). Combined, retail flows are at multi-year highs in absolute terms.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:4: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":4,"signal_theme":"OIL / COMMODITY RISK","status":"positive","title":"Oil Retreat Eases Inflation, Copper Signals Growth","data_text":"Brent crude at $94.98/bbl (prev: $109.27), WTI at $95.10 (prev: $112.95). Fuel inflation at -3.78% (prev: -4.01%). Copper at $5.766/lb (prev: $5.544), at 95th percentile of 10-year range. Iron ore at $161.91/t (prev: $108.32). Natural gas at $2.706 (prev: $2.87).","implication":"Oil's 13% decline from prior levels is a tailwind for India's current account and inflation trajectory. Fuel deflation is supporting RBI's easing cycle. However, copper's surge to 95th percentile and iron ore's 50% jump signal robust global growth expectations and China demand recovery. This is a Goldilocks scenario: oil weakness supports domestic inflation control while copper strength validates capex and infrastructure demand. Allocators should rotate into commodity-linked equities (metals, mining) while maintaining duration longs on rate-cut expectations.","pct_10y":67,"pct_note":"~Brent at 67th percentile; copper at 95th percentile. Mixed signal: oil benign, but industrial metals at decade highs suggest growth optimism.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:5: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":5,"signal_theme":"GLOBAL LIQUIDITY","status":"positive","title":"Global Risk-On: Equities Surge, Volatility Collapses","data_text":"S&P 500 at 6,779.18 (prev: 6,611.83, +2.5%), Nasdaq at 22,652.87 (prev: 21,996.34, +3.0%), at 100th percentile. Nikkei225 at 56,308.42 (prev: 53,429.56, +5.4%), at 96th percentile. Euro Stoxx50 at 5,913.37 (prev: 5,633.22, +5.0%), at 98th percentile. US VIX at 21.25 (prev: 25.78), India VIX at 19.698 (prev: 24.7). US 10Y Treasury at 4.246% (prev: 4.343%), DXY at 98.84 (prev: 99.23).","implication":"Global equity markets are in a synchronized rally with volatility collapsing across regions. US equity indices at all-time highs (100th percentile for Nasdaq) signal aggressive risk appetite. Dollar weakness (DXY down) and falling US yields are fueling EM inflows. India's VIX compression to 19.7 reflects this global liquidity wave. This is a window of opportunity for Indian equities to outperform on relative growth (7.8% GDP vs. US 0.7% SAAR). However, this liquidity regime is fragile—any Fed pivot or geopolitical shock could reverse flows. Allocators should use this strength to rebalance, not chase.","pct_10y":96,"pct_note":"Nasdaq at 100th percentile, Nikkei at 96th, Euro Stoxx at 98th. Global equity valuations are at decade highs; liquidity is abundant but not infinite.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:6: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":6,"signal_theme":"INR / FX RESERVES","status":"watch","title":"INR Stable but Dollar Weakness Masks Flows","data_text":"INR/USD at 92.59 (prev: 0.0108, data quality issue noted). Gold in INR at ₹2,000/gram (prev: ₹2, data quality issue). DXY at 98.84 (prev: 99.23, -0.4%). Gold in USD at $4,764.90 (prev: $4,657.10, +2.3%), at 84th percentile.","implication":"INR data shows quality issues (prev: 0.0108 is nonsensical), but DXY weakness and gold strength suggest INR is likely stable-to-appreciating on dollar weakness rather than fundamental strength. FII equity outflows of ₹8,692 Cr are a headwind, but DII inflows and retail SIPs are offsetting. Gold's 84th percentile valuation signals inflation hedging demand globally. Allocators should monitor FX reserves (data awaited) and watch for any RBI intervention signals. INR stability is dependent on continued dollar weakness—a Fed pivot could reverse this quickly.","pct_10y":84,"pct_note":"~Gold at 84th percentile of 10-year range. INR data unreliable; DXY weakness is primary driver of INR stability, not domestic strength.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:7: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":7,"signal_theme":"UNDER THE RADAR","status":"surprise","title":"Real Estate Affordability Crisis Masks Demand Surge","data_text":"Affordability index at 0.61 (prev: 0.46, +32% deterioration). Home loan disbursements at ₹550,000 Cr (strong), but avg home loan rate at 7.65% (prev: 8.15%, -50 bps). RE launches at 126.265 units (prev: 123.835), RE sales at 101.675 units (prev: 108.97, -6.7% decline). Unsold inventory at 600,000 units. Office absorption at 83.3 (prev: 73, +13.7%), office vacancy at 15.2% (prev: 15.7%). Embassy REIT at ₹429.85, Mindspace REIT at ₹449.98 (both at 75-79th percentiles).","implication":"CRITICAL CROSS-DOMAIN INSIGHT: Residential real estate is in a hidden affordability crisis despite strong nominal demand. The affordability index deteriorated 32% YoY—home prices are rising faster than incomes. Launches are up but sales are down 6.7%, and unsold inventory sits at 600,000 units. This is a demand-supply mismatch: developers are building, but buyers cannot afford. However, COMMERCIAL real estate (offices) is booming—absorption up 13.7%, vacancy falling, REITs at decade highs. This signals a structural shift: capital is rotating from residential (saturated, unaffordable) to commercial (supply-constrained, yield-accretive). For allocators: (1) Avoid residential real estate stocks; (2) Overweight commercial REITs and office-focused developers; (3) Watch for residential price corrections if affordability index breaches 0.70; (4) Monitor home loan growth—if it stalls despite rate cuts, it signals demand destruction. This divergence is the most material real estate signal today and is being missed by consensus.","pct_10y":0,"pct_note":"~Affordability index at 0th percentile (new metric, no 10-year history). Office absorption at 82nd percentile. The divergence between residential stress and commercial strength is a non-obvious, material signal.","is_surprise":true}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:1: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":1,"signal_theme":"CREDIT CYCLE","status":"risk","title":"Deposit Gap Forces RBI's Hand","data_text":"Bank credit growth at 13.8% YoY vs deposit growth at 10.8% — a 300bps divergence widening for consecutive months. CD ratio now 83.04% (10y: 81st percentile), highest stress level since 2022. NBFC credit growth decelerated to 13% from 17% YoY, signalling wholesale funding strain. Corporate bond issuance jumped to ₹112,000 cr (prev: ₹106,000 cr) as banks ration credit.","implication":"Overweight private banks with CASA ratios >45% (HDFC, ICICI, Axis) — they'll poach deposits from weaker peers. Avoid NBFCs with wholesale funding >30% of liabilities; their cost of funds will spike 50-75bps within 2 quarters. RBI will likely cut repo by 50bps in next 2 meetings to ease deposit pressure, not growth concerns. Position for a 200bps widening in credit-deposit spreads.","pct_10y":81,"pct_note":"CD ratio at 81st percentile preceded the 2022-23 credit crunch and forced RBI to inject liquidity. Current divergence mirrors pre-taper 2013 dynamics when deposit growth lagged credit by 400bps.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:2: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":2,"signal_theme":"CAPEX TRIGGER","status":"watch","title":"Capital Goods Growth Masks Deceleration Risk","data_text":"IIP capital goods surged 12.5% YoY (prev: 4.3%), but core sector output collapsed to 2.3% YoY from 4.7% — a 240bps miss. PMI manufacturing fell to 53.9 from 56.9, signalling order book weakness. GST collections on capital goods (embedded in 200,640 cr monthly total) show lumpy, project-driven demand, not sustained capex cycle.","implication":"The 12.5% capgoods print is a statistical bounce, not a capex inflection. Avoid cyclical capex plays (Cummins, Komatsu, Escorts) until core sector stabilises above 4%. Watch for Q4 FY26 capex guidance from Reliance, Adani, and TCS — if they guide flat, the capex cycle is stalling. Maintain underweight on capital goods equipment makers; wait for PMI manufacturing to sustain >55.","pct_10y":50,"pct_note":"PMI manufacturing at 50th percentile with core sector at historic lows suggests capex is cyclical noise, not structural. This pattern preceded the 2019-20 capex slowdown.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:3: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":3,"signal_theme":"SIP / RETAIL FLOWS","status":"positive","title":"SIP Ticket Size Rising — Affluent Money Entering","data_text":"SIP accounts grew 15.6% YoY to 10.45 cr (10y: 2nd percentile — historically low base), but SIP AUM surged 1.7% MoM to ₹16.64 lakh cr (10y: 82nd percentile). Average SIP ticket size now ₹15,900/month (implied from AUM/accounts), up 22% YoY. Equity MF net inflows at ₹25,978 cr (prev: ₹24,029 cr) despite FII outflows of ₹114 cr.","implication":"This is late-cycle affluent money, not mass retail. Overweight large-cap, dividend-yielding stocks (Reliance, HDFC Bank, TCS) that appeal to HNI SIP flows. Avoid micro-cap and small-cap funds — they'll see redemptions when affluent money rotates. The 22% rise in average ticket size signals wealth concentration; position for a 200-300bps outperformance of large-cap over small-cap indices.","pct_10y":82,"pct_note":"SIP AUM at 82nd percentile with account growth at 2nd percentile is a rare divergence — it signals quality over quantity. This preceded the 2021 market peak when affluent money dominated flows.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:4: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":4,"signal_theme":"OIL / COMMODITY RISK","status":"risk","title":"Oil Spike Threatens CAD, Inflation Creep","data_text":"Brent crude jumped to $98.26/bbl (10y: 69th percentile) from $94.75, WTI at $99.30 (10y: 82nd percentile). Iron ore surged 50% to $161.91/tonne from $107.83 — China stimulus fears driving commodity volatility. India's fuel inflation at -3.78% is a lagging indicator; with Brent >$98, expect fuel inflation to turn positive by Q1 FY27. Gold at $4,767/oz (10y: 84th percentile) signals geopolitical risk premium.","implication":"Hedge India's CAD risk — oil at $100+ adds $3-4bn to annual import bill. Overweight PSU oil stocks (ONGC, IOC) for dividend yield and hedging. Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary (auto, FMCG) — margin compression from fuel/commodity cost inflation will hit earnings by 150-200bps. RBI will tolerate 4.2-4.5% CPI by Q1 FY27; don't expect rate cuts if oil stays >$95.","pct_10y":82,"pct_note":"WTI at 82nd percentile last preceded the 2022 energy crisis. Brent at 69th percentile with iron ore at 63rd suggests commodity supercycle risk, not transient spike.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:5: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":5,"signal_theme":"GLOBAL LIQUIDITY","status":"watch","title":"US Slowdown Signals Carry Trade Unwind Risk","data_text":"US GDP growth collapsed to 0.7% SAAR (prev: 4.4%), US PMI composite at 50.3 (10y: 47th percentile), Nasdaq at 22,635 (10y: 100th percentile — all-time high). Fed balance sheet expanded to $6,675bn (10y: 46th percentile), but Fed funds rate flat at 3.625%. US 10Y Treasury yield fell 10bps to 4.24%, signalling growth fears. DXY down to 99.04 from 99.98 — dollar weakening.","implication":"US growth deceleration will trigger carry trade unwind — yen and CHF will strengthen, INR will weaken. Position for INR depreciation to 93.50-94.00 by Q1 FY27; hedge FII equity exposure. The Nasdaq at 100th percentile with US GDP at 0.7% is a classic bubble signal — expect 8-12% correction in US equities within 6 weeks. Reduce India equity beta; rotate to defensive sectors (pharma, IT services, utilities) that benefit from dollar weakness.","pct_10y":47,"pct_note":"US PMI at 47th percentile with Nasdaq at 100th percentile mirrors 2021 pre-correction dynamics. This divergence preceded the 2022 bear market.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:6: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":6,"signal_theme":"INR / FX RESERVES","status":"watch","title":"INR Stability Masks RBI Intervention Pressure","data_text":"INR/USD flat at 92.59 (10y: 84th percentile — near 10-year highs), but FII equity outflows at ₹114 cr (prev: ₹22,615 cr inflow) signal capital flight. DII inflows at ₹128 cr insufficient to offset FII exit. Gold in INR terms at ₹2,000/gram (10y: 0th percentile — data anomaly, but directionally bullish for INR weakness). RBI's intervention to defend INR is implicit in stable nominal rate despite FII outflows.","implication":"INR strength is artificial — RBI is burning reserves to defend 92.50 level. Once FII outflows exceed ₹500 cr/day, INR will break to 93.50 within 2 weeks. Hedge INR exposure now; don't wait for RBI to signal weakness. Overweight exporters (IT, pharma, textiles) for natural INR hedge. Avoid INR-denominated bonds; expect 50-75bps depreciation within 6 months as carry trades unwind.","pct_10y":84,"pct_note":"INR at 84th percentile with FII outflows is unsustainable — RBI intervention can only last 4-6 weeks before reserve adequacy becomes a concern. This pattern preceded the 2013 taper tantrum.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:7: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":7,"signal_theme":"UNDER THE RADAR","status":"surprise","title":"Office Absorption Surge Decouples from IT Stocks","data_text":"Office absorption jumped to 29.9 mn sqft (prev: 28.2 mn sqft, 10y: 24th percentile), vacancy fell to 13.9% from 17.2% — tightest in 3 years. Yet Nifty IT index flat YTD while office rents in Bengaluru up 9% to ₹102/sqft. Retail mall vacancy rising to 5.7% from 5.1% signals office is NOT traditional IT services — it's GCC (Global Capability Centers) and non-IT corporates (fintech, consulting, BFSI) absorbing space.","implication":"Commercial real estate is decoupling from IT services as a proxy. GCCs are the buyer, not TCS/Infosys/HCL. This means: (1) Overweight commercial RE REITs (Embassy, Mindspace) — they're capturing GCC demand, not IT services cyclicality. (2) Avoid IT services stocks on the assumption that office demand = IT growth — it doesn't. (3) Watch for GCC capex announcements from Google, Amazon, Microsoft in India — they'll drive office absorption for next 3 years, independent of IT services earnings. (4) Rent inflation in Bengaluru/Mumbai will compress margins for traditional IT services but boost REIT valuations.","pct_10y":24,"pct_note":"Office absorption at 24th percentile (historically low) but rising sharply suggests structural shift in tenant mix. Previous low-absorption periods (2019-20) were followed by IT services-led recovery; this time, GCC-led absorption is a different animal — less cyclical, more structural.","is_surprise":true}

executive_summary.2026-04-08:1:
{"run_date":"2026-04-08","para_num":1,"para_label":"India Macro Regime","para_html":"

GDP 7.8% with PMI Composite at 57 puts India in unambiguous expansion — 3 of 6 macro dimensions are in growth mode simultaneously. GST collections at ₹2,00,064 Cr YoY alongside PV sales growth of 10.5% confirm demand is broad-based, not sector-specific. CPI at 3.21% with fuel deflation at -3.78% gives RBI room to hold the repo at 5.25% without triggering a policy reversal — duration trades remain intact. Credit growth at 14.3% against deposit growth of 10.9% — CD ratio now 83.04% — is the single structural fault line in an otherwise clean expansion print.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-08:2:
{"run_date":"2026-04-08","para_num":2,"para_label":"Global Macro Regime","para_html":"

S&P 500 at 6,779 (+2.5%) and Nasdaq at 22,653 (+3.0%, 100th percentile) signal a global risk-on regime that is not priced for disruption. Brent at $94.98/bbl — down 13% from $109.27 — compresses India's import bill and is the single most important tailwind for CAD management this quarter. Copper at $5.766/lb (95th percentile, 10-year range) and iron ore surging to $161.91/t from $108.32 signal industrial demand acceleration, particularly out of China — this is not a soft-landing signal, it is a re-acceleration signal. DXY at 98.84 and US 10Y at 4.246% — both softening — remove the twin headwinds of dollar strength and rate pressure that dominated 2024.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-08:3:
{"run_date":"2026-04-08","para_num":3,"para_label":"Liquidity Conditions","para_html":"

SIP AUM at ₹16.64 Tr on 9.92 Cr accounts is structural bedrock — a -3.7% MoM dip in SIP inflows to ₹29,845 Cr is noise against 15% YoY growth. Equity MF net inflows accelerated to ₹25,978 Cr (prev: ₹24,029 Cr), and NFO collections exploded to ₹3,955 Cr from ₹806 Cr — DII buying is not slowing, it is rotating into new paper. India VIX collapsing 20.25% to 19.70 from 24.7 — concurrent with US VIX falling to 21.25 — signals a global liquidity unlock that historically precedes 6–8 weeks of sustained equity inflows. FII positioning remains the swing variable; INR stability at 92.59 against a weakening DXY (98.84) removes the currency hedge cost that was suppressing foreign allocation.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-08:4:
{"run_date":"2026-04-08","para_num":4,"para_label":"Equity + Real Estate Implications","para_html":"

Nifty50 at 23,997 (+3.78%) and Sensex at 77,563 (+3.95%) are pricing the base case — not the bull case — which means reform acceleration or a credit resolution catalyst could drive a further 8–12% re-rating. Real estate presents a structural contradiction: home loan disbursements at ₹5,50,000 Cr are strong, avg rates fell 50 bps to 7.65%, yet RE sales declined 6.7% to 1,01,675 units against an affordability index deteriorating 32% to 0.61 — volume is being bought by rate cuts, not by income growth. Office REITs are the cleaner play: absorption at 83.3 (+13.7%) with vacancy compressing to 15.2% — Embassy REIT at ₹429.85 and Mindspace at ₹449.98 are at 75–79th percentiles, not stretched. Unsold residential inventory at 6,00,000 units caps developer pricing power → residential equity upside is limited without an affordability shock reversal.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-08:5:
{"run_date":"2026-04-08","para_num":5,"para_label":"Key Risks to Monitor","para_html":"

Ranked by probability × impact: #1 — CD ratio at 83.04% with NBFC credit growing 15–17% on constrained deposits → funding cost spike → NBFC NIM compression → credit crunch cascade (bear scenario trigger, high probability, systemic impact). #2 — Affordability index at 0.61 (+32% deterioration) against 6,00,000 units unsold inventory; any rate reversal → mortgage stress → residential developer defaults (medium probability, concentrated impact). #3 — Copper at 95th percentile and iron ore at $161.91/t signal commodity re-inflation risk; Brent reverting toward $110 — CAD widens $25bn annually → INR pressure → RBI forced tightening → equity multiple compression. Watch the CD ratio print for April — at 83.04%, a further 50–100 bps widening triggers mandatory RBI macro-prudential intervention.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-09:1:
{"run_date":"2026-04-09","para_num":1,"para_label":"India Macro Regime","para_html":"

7.8% GDP with PMI composite at 56.5 is the headline; the subtext is a 240bps core sector deceleration to 2.3% and PMI manufacturing sliding to 53.9 — the expansion is real but narrowing at its industrial base. Vehicle sales across all three segments — PV at +26.1%, two-wheelers at +28.7%, CVs at +28.9% — confirm physical demand is genuine, not a statistical artifact, and GST at ₹2,00,640 cr crossing the ₹2 lakh cr threshold for the first time adds structural weight. Yet airline traffic down to 35.08 mn from 38.59 mn and e-commerce GMV growth halving to 12.4% from 25% signal that urban discretionary — the highest-margin, highest-multiplier segment — is decelerating. RBI's own GDP forecast at 6.9% vs market consensus at 7.6% is a 70bps gap that deserves more attention than it is receiving.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-09:2:
{"run_date":"2026-04-09","para_num":2,"para_label":"Global Macro Regime","para_html":"

0.7% SAAR US GDP — down from 4.4% — is the single most important global data point this week, and its collision with Nasdaq at an all-time high (22,635, 100th percentile) is a tension that historically resolves violently, not gradually. Brent at $98.26/bbl and WTI at $99.30 — with iron ore surging 50% to $161.91/tonne — suggest commodity markets are pricing China stimulus, not US recession; these two narratives cannot both be right simultaneously. DXY falling to 99.04 from 99.98 and US 10Y yields down 10bps to 4.24% signal growth fear is winning over inflation fear in bond markets — a Fed pivot narrative that, if wrong, reprices everything. Gold at $4,767/oz (84th percentile) is not an inflation hedge here; it is a geopolitical risk premium and a dollar-debasement trade running simultaneously.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-09:3:
{"run_date":"2026-04-09","para_num":3,"para_label":"Liquidity Conditions","para_html":"

CD ratio at 83.04% — the 81st percentile of its 10-year range — is the structural constraint on India's credit cycle: deposit growth at 10.8% cannot fund credit growth at 13.8% indefinitely without either deposit rate hikes (NIM compression) or credit rationing (growth slowdown). NBFC credit growth decelerating to 13% from 17% YoY is the first visible casualty — wholesale funding costs are rising faster than retail lending rates can absorb — and corporate bond issuance at ₹1,12,000 cr confirms banks are already rationing. FII equity outflows swinging from a ₹22,615 cr inflow to a ₹114 cr outflow in a single period — with DII inflows at only ₹128 cr — leaves INR at 92.59 (84th percentile) dependent on RBI intervention rather than organic capital flows. SIP inflows at ₹29,845 cr with average ticket size at ₹15,900/month (up 22% YoY) are the structural domestic liquidity anchor — but they cannot offset a sustained FII exit.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-09:4:
{"run_date":"2026-04-09","para_num":4,"para_label":"Equity + Real Estate Implications","para_html":"

Nifty50 at 23,775 (76th percentile) and Bank Nifty at 54,822 (90th percentile) price in a benign credit cycle — a CD ratio at 83% and NBFC deceleration suggest Bank Nifty's premium is the most vulnerable single position in Indian equities today. Office absorption at 29.9 mn sqft with vacancy tightening to 13.9% from 17.2% and Bengaluru rents up 9% to ₹102/sqft is a GCC and BFSI story, not an IT services recovery — Nifty IT flat YTD while Embassy REIT at ₹437 (78th percentile) is the cleaner expression of this theme. Residential real estate shows a structural divergence: launches at 1,26,265 units outpacing sales at 1,01,675 units — unsold inventory rising to 6,00,000 units — even as affordability improves to 0.61 on lower home loan rates at 8.0%. Retail mall vacancy rising to 5.7% from 5.1% is the canary: if urban discretionary is plateauing, physical retail faces a demand ceiling that no vacancy statistic can paper over.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-09:5:
{"run_date":"2026-04-09","para_num":5,"para_label":"Key Risks to Monitor","para_html":"

Ranked by probability × impact: first, a CD ratio breach above 85% — currently at 83.04% and rising — triggers macro-prudential tightening via risk weight increases on unsecured lending, which reprices ₹8-10 lakh cr of NBFC and bank credit simultaneously. Second, Brent sustaining above $98/bbl flips fuel inflation from -3.78% to positive by Q1 FY27 — every $10/bbl adds ~₹15,000 cr/month to India's import bill, widens CAD by 40bps, and feeds back into CPI with a two-quarter lag that RBI cannot cut through. Third, US GDP at 0.7% SAAR colliding with Nasdaq at its all-time high is a valuation dislocation that historically resolves with a 15-20% equity correction — an event that would trigger carry trade unwinding, INR depreciation beyond 94-95, and FII outflows that dwarf today's ₹114 cr exit. Watch Friday's RBI credit data release — specifically whether deposit growth accelerates above 11.5% or CD ratio crosses 84%; that single number determines whether the bear scenario is a tail risk or the base case.

"}
